averageAUM.description: Average % of assets under management charged by SA financial advisors
averageAUM.note: This is ON TOP of fund TERs. So a 1% advisor fee + 1.6% fund TER = 2.6% total.

feeByPortfolioSize.note: Small portfolios get hit hardest percentage-wise
Advisors make great money here, negotiate hard
Should be getting premium service for these fees
High net worth - fees should be rock bottom

feeOnlyAdvisors.description: Fee-only advisors charge flat fees instead of % of assets
feeOnlyAdvisors.note: Fee-only is almost always cheaper for portfolios over R500k. An R800k portfolio paying 1% AUM = R8,000/year — same as a full annual plan from a fee-only advisor.

totalCostExample.scenario: R800,000 portfolio with a typical advisor

cheapAlternative.fund: Satrix Balanced Index Fund via EasyEquities

redFlags: Advisor fee above 1.5% on any portfolio size
No annual fee disclosure statement provided
Switching funds frequently (churning) to generate fees
Recommending funds from only one provider (likely getting higher commission)
Not explaining performance fees or hidden costs
Recommending wrap funds (fund-of-funds) that add another layer of fees
Charging initial fees (upfront deductions from your investment)
Not registered with the FSCA or no FSP number
Pushing life insurance products you don't need
No written record of advice (IPS - Investment Policy Statement)
yourRights: Right to a clear fee disclosure BEFORE investing (FAIS Act)
Right to an annual fee statement showing all costs
Right to switch advisors or go DIY at any time
Right to complain to FAIS Ombud if treated unfairly
Right to see your advisor's FSP number and verify registration
Right to know if your advisor earns commission from product providers
Right to a suitability analysis (advice must match your needs)
Right to cooling-off period on new investments

faisOmbud.name: FAIS Ombud (Financial Advisory and Intermediary Services)
faisOmbud.note: Free to use. Must first complain to the advisor/company directly. If not resolved within 6 weeks, escalate to FAIS Ombud.
